messages.subject: [R-SIG-Win] Rtools 3.5
messages.body_plain:
Hi, Jeroen.

Maybe I wasn't paying attention, but is there anywhere which explains
what was changed from Rtools 3.4 to 3.5?

Thanks,

Avi
messages.body_snippet:
Hi, Jeroen.

Maybe I wasn't paying attention, but is there anywhere which explains
what was changed from Rtools 3.4 to 3.5?

Thanks,

Avi

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Win] Rtools 3.5
messages.body_plain:
On Thu, Apr 12, 2018 at 6:31 AM, Avraham Adler <avraham.adler at gmail.com> wrote:
>
> Maybe I wasn't paying attention, but is there anywhere which explains
> what was changed from Rtools 3.4 to 3.5?

The toolchain is unchanged, but I updated the build utilities (make,
sh, tar, etc) and refactored the installer. The scripts are now
available at: https://github.com/rwinlib/rtools35

FYI we are planning a big toolchain update based on gcc 8 for this summer.

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Win] Rtools 3.5
messages.body_plain:
Should we add qpdf too? They?re up to 8.0.2 now.

Also, for the record, I just installed 3.5 with new tool chain and OpenBLAS
0.2.20 and it passes make check-devel :)

Thanks,

Avi

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Win] Rtools 3.5
messages.body_plain:
Btw. if we are already talking about aspell, how about using hunspell instead?
AFAIR (but FIXME) aspell for windows is long unmaintained.

R supports hunspell as well, although as I remember, a slight change in needed
in the check code, because that is hardwired to aspell.

Gabor

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Win] Rtools 3.5
messages.body_plain:
On Tue, Apr 24, 2018 at 10:01 PM, G?bor Cs?rdi <csardi.gabor at gmail.com> wrote:
> Btw. if we are already talking about aspell, how about using hunspell instead?
> AFAIR (but FIXME) aspell for windows is long unmaintained.

We use the standard aspell branch (0.60.7-rc1), not the special win32
port (which seems dead). I built a standalone version earlier this
year: https://github.com/rwinlib/aspell

This is what CRAN currently runs, so I would like to give users the
same results.

Actually, how do I run CMD check with spell checking? I tried setting my
.Renviron to:

 _R_CHECK_CRAN_INCOMING_USE_ASPELL_=1

But that doesn't seem to work. I also can't any information on
https://cran.r-project.org/doc/manuals/r-release/R-ints.html

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Win] Rtools 3.5
messages.body_plain:
On 25.04.2018 19:53, G?bor Cs?rdi wrote:
> I think  you need --as-cran as well. G.

I have

set _R_CHECK_CRAN_INCOMING_=TRUE
set _R_CHECK_CRAN_INCOMING_USE_ASPELL_=TRUE


(and some more) butnot --as-cran which I never use myself as I have more 
enabled anyway.

Best,
Uwe
